assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md contains a 'Feature Ambiguities Identified' table listing 6 ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' - no latency target specified, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' - no definition of relevant, no ranking criteria, (3) 'Add filters' - no filter dimensions specified, (4) 'Better UI' - no Figma design, no specific improvements, (5) 'Should be fast enough' - no quantitative performance requirement, (6) 'Don't break existing functionality' - no specific concerns raised. This exceeds the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md ambiguity table row for 'Better UI' states: 'Marked as non-MVP. No Figma design provided. No specific UI improvements described.' with the planning assumption: 'Excluded from backend planning — this is a frontend concern marked non-MVP.' No task addresses Better UI.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks follow the template format with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify/Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Verification Commands, and Dependencies. This matches the task-description-template.md structure.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: While the impact-map.md documents ambiguities and 'Planning assumptions' in its table, these are labeled as 'Planning assumption' not 'assumptions pending clarification'. The individual task descriptions do not contain any sections or labels marking assumptions as pending clarification. The impact map says 'In a real engagement, these would be raised with the product owner before proceeding' but does not use the 'pending clarification' label.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain a '## Repository' section with exactly 'trustify-backend' as the single repository name: task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4, task-6 line 4.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths across all 6 tasks are present in the repo-backend.md manifest: migration/src/lib.rs, modules/search/src/lib.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/search.rs. Files to Create paths follow the manifest's established directory structure and naming patterns: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the pattern from migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs; modules/search/src/model/mod.rs and modules/search/src/model/summary.rs follow the model/ directory pattern from modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ and modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/. No paths reference directories or structures inconsistent with the manifest.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have Implementation Notes referencing specific file paths and patterns: Task 1 references 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs'. Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary)', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs (AdvisorySummary)', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs'. Task 3 references 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs::AppError'. Task 4 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 5 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 6 references 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 7, task-2 line 7, task-3 line 7, task-4 line 7, task-5 line 7, task-6 line 7.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: None of the output files contain any reference to 'sha256', 'digest', 'Description digest', or '[sdlc-workflow]'. No digest comments are present in any of the 7 output files (impact-map.md and task-1 through task-6).

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: None of the 6 task descriptions contain any reference to 'CONVENTIONS.md', 'Per CONVENTIONS.md', or 'Applies:' rationale lines. The convention-aware enrichment process is completely absent from all task outputs. No convention applicability analysis is documented anywhere in the output files.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature has Priority: Normal (not Undefined). The impact-map.md states 'Priority: Normal (propagated to all tasks)' under 'Fields Inherited from Feature'. However, none of the 6 task descriptions contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of 'priority' being set as a field. The priority inheritance is documented only at the impact map level, not in the actual task output format that would be sent to Jira.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0. There is no fixVersion scope config in CLAUDE.md, so it defaults to 'both', meaning tasks should include fixVersions. The impact-map.md states 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (propagated to all tasks)'. However, none of the 6 task descriptions contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of 'fixVersions' being set. The fixVersion inheritance is documented only at the impact map level, not in the actual task output.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: There is no summary comment output file in the outputs directory. The impact-map.md mentions 'Priority: Normal (propagated to all tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (propagated to all tasks)' under 'Fields Inherited from Feature', but there is no dedicated summary comment file that would represent a comment posted on the feature issue (Step 6c). The outputs only contain impact-map.md and task-1 through task-6.
